text: I find myself in a sleek, modern space defined by glass and careful design. The environment is clean, organized, and almost clinical, invoking a sense of curiosity and a quiet reverence for human innovation. Walking from the corridor into the exhibit marked 'R+D,' I feel a subtle thrill. Here, intellect and creativity converge, and I am drawn to ponder not just what is displayed, but why it matters—how the research and development of today shapes our understanding of existence tomorrow. I feel alert, contemplative, and a bit exhilarated by the presence of so much thought made tangible.
